channel.title: NPR Topics: Business
channel.description: Find the latest business news with reports on Wall Street, interest rates, banking, companies, and U.S. and world financial markets. Subscribe to the Business Story of the Day podcast.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Trump's name is still on the Kennedy Center — officials say it will be down by noon
item.description: President Donald Trump's name remained on the facade of the Kennedy Center early Saturday despite a court-ordered Friday deadline to remove references to Trump from the building.
item.content:encoded:
President Donald Trump's name remained on the facade of the Kennedy Center early Saturday despite a court-ordered Friday deadline to remove references to Trump from the building.

(Image credit: Cliff Owen)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Paramount-Warner Brothers merger gets Justice Department approval
item.description: The Justice Department closed its investigation into the proposed $110 billion merger of Paramount and Warner Brothers Discovery, saying it found no threat to competition or consumers.
item.content:encoded: The Justice Department closed its investigation into the proposed $110 billion merger of Paramount and Warner Brothers Discovery, saying it found no threat to competition or consumers.
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik

item.title: Justice Dept. approves Paramount's acquisition of Warner Bros. Discovery
item.description: The decision paves the way for a proposed $111 billion merger uniting two rival studio giants — Paramount, owner of CBS, and the much larger Warner, which includes HBO and CNN.
item.content:encoded:
The decision paves the way for a proposed $111 billion merger uniting two rival studio giants — Paramount, owner of CBS, and the much larger Warner, which includes HBO and CNN.

(Image credit: Nick Ut)
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik

item.title: SpaceX IPO makes history as largest ever. Stock gains 19% on first day
item.description: The initial public offering from the rocket and AI company raised some $75 billion, making the company one of the biggest in the world — and likely making Elon Musk a trillionaire.
item.content:encoded:
The initial public offering from the rocket and AI company raised some $75 billion, making the company one of the biggest in the world — and likely making Elon Musk a trillionaire.

(Image credit: RONALDO SCHEMIDT)
item.dc:creator: John Ruwitch

item.title: Door shuts on some immigrant entrepreneurs as U.S. restricts small-business loans
item.description: For decades, immigrants who are legal permanent residents in the U.S. could get loans through the Small Business Administration, a core pillar of small-business lending. Not anymore.
item.content:encoded:
For decades, immigrants who are legal permanent residents in the U.S. could get loans through the Small Business Administration, a core pillar of small-business lending. Not anymore.

(Image credit: Courtesy of Sayuri Tsuchitani)
item.dc:creator: Alina Selyukh

item.title: Can smartphones help explain the drop in birth rates?
item.description: Are smartphones causing people to have fewer children? A provocative new working paper explores the persistent drop in birth rates since the iPhone was introduced nearly two decades ago.
item.content:encoded:
Are smartphones causing people to have fewer children? A provocative new working paper explores the persistent drop in birth rates since the iPhone was introduced nearly two decades ago.

(Image credit: David Paul Morris)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: SpaceX IPO expected to set record when it goes public Friday
item.description: SpaceX will go public on the NASDAQ Friday, likely to set a record as the most valuable IPO in history. NASDAQ is making a big play for IPOs, with new rules aimed at drawing in companies like SpaceX.
item.content:encoded: SpaceX will go public on the NASDAQ Friday, likely to set a record as the most valuable IPO in history. NASDAQ is making a big play for IPOs, with new rules aimed at drawing in companies like SpaceX.
item.dc:creator: Ricky Mulvey

item.title: How companies can sneak new chemicals past the FDA — and into our food and supplements
item.description: There's a sneaky way companies add new chemicals in our food, and it is there by design, and totally legal.
item.content:encoded: There's a sneaky way companies add new chemicals in our food, and it is there by design, and totally legal.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Gonzalez

item.title: Small Business Administration stops lending to green card holders
item.description: For decades, immigrants who are legal permanent residents in the U.S. could get loans through the Small Business Administration, a core pillar of small-business lending. Not anymore.
item.content:encoded: For decades, immigrants who are legal permanent residents in the U.S. could get loans through the Small Business Administration, a core pillar of small-business lending. Not anymore.
item.dc:creator: Alina Selyukh

item.title: SpaceX blasts off with a record-breaking $75 billion IPO
item.description: Elon Musk's rocket company, recently merged with xAI, raised $75 billion in its initial public stock offering. It's the first of a trio of mega-IPOs from AI companies expected this year.
item.content:encoded:
Elon Musk's rocket company, recently merged with xAI, raised $75 billion in its initial public stock offering. It's the first of a trio of mega-IPOs from AI companies expected this year.

(Image credit: Eric Gay)
item.dc:creator: John Ruwitch
